title: Tarisian military base

Once a [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] military base where scientists sought to find a vaccine for the [[Rakghoul plague/Legends|rakghoul plague]], the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith]] captured the '''Tarisian military base''' when they took control of the planet. It became the headquarters of the Sith government on the planet, led by a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Sith Governor]].

In addition to the governor's quarters, the base also featured detention facilities for enemies of the Sith, barracks for the [[Sith trooper (Sith Empire)|Sith soldiers]] stationed on [[Taris/Legends|Taris]], and facilities to repair and maintain the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] that provided base security.

[[File:KT-400_Taris_Sith_Base.png|thumb|left|150px|A [[KT-400 military droid carrier]] landing at the base.]]
The base was the site of a [[Rescue of Bastila Shan|break-in]] in [[3956 BBY]] when the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Canderous Ordo]] approached [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] about obtaining the launch codes needed to bypass the Sith blockade of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. In return, Canderous would help Revan steal the ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'', the ship of Canderous's employer [[Davik Kang]], and escape from the planet. Revan purchased a droid named [[T3-M4]] from [[Janice Nall]] and used it to break into the base. Once inside, Revan came face-to-face with the Sith Governor, who sensed [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] within Revan. Seeing an opportunity to receive his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], the Sith Governor attacked Revan, but was soon [[Death/Legends|killed]]. Revan took the launch codes from the Governor's quarters and brought them to Canderous.

==Behind the scenes==
This military base was solely seen in the [[2003]] video game ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', during the period which [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] and [[Carth Onasi]] are stranded on Taris, while in search of [[Bastila Shan]].

There is a glitch in the game, where in one of the corridors, a single blaster is seen lying on the floor, however it isn't involved with the plot, nor can any character pick it up or acknowledge it.
